event: transported to our hospital by ambulance
hemorrhage in the bilateral lateral ventricles
arterial phase: source of bleeding unclear
venous phase: spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
aneurysm (3.8 mm) at the distal site of the mLSA arising from the proximal A1 segment of the left ACA
guiding sheath navigated into the petrous portion of the left internal carotid artery
compression study of the right common carotid artery confirmed the anterior communicating artery (AComA)
guiding sheath introduced into the petrous portion of the right internal carotid artery
Marathon advanced through AComA into mLSA origin but unable to be advanced near the aneurysm
changed to DeFrictor nano catheter and TENROU S10 guide wire
perforating artery distal and proximal to the aneurysm filled with NBCA
angiography after NBCA injection: disappearance of the aneurysm
postoperative head CT: no hemorrhagic complications
postoperative head CT: no cerebral infarction
neither impaired consciousness nor paralysis developed
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
MRI evaluation not possible (biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator implanted)
decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
